title: erica abietina subsp. abietina
intro: Erica abietina subsp. abietina, also known as the alder heath, is a sub-species of the Erica abietina plant, which is a low-growing, woody shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as alleviate symptoms of colds and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Erica abietina subsp. abietina include its ability to support immune function, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote overall well-being. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
